answer:
verdict: bottom-of-funnel content 1st, CTA fixes 2nd, skip gating

why:
- 0.5% blog→trial isn't bad tbh, typical is 0.3-1%. real issue is prob traffic mix: 40k visits mostly top-of-funnel/informational readers who were never gonna sign up. no CTA redesign fixes intent mismatch
- BOFU content (comparisons, "X alternative", pricing/use-case pages, integration pages) converts 5-25x better per visit. even 2k BOFU visits/mo at 5% = 100 trials vs ur current ~200 from 40k. best ROI per hr of work
- CTA redesign = worth doing but its a multiplier on weak base. do cheap wins: contextual inline CTAs matched to post topic instead of generic banner, add CTAs mid-post not just end. maybe +20-50% lift, so 200→250-300 signups. fine but capped
- gating = worst option 4 u. gates get emails, not trials. adds friction, kills SEO value of ur best pages (gated content doesnt rank), + u'd then need email nurture machinery to convert leads→trials. only makes sense w/ sales-led motion, not PLG trial signups

plan: audit which existing posts already drive signups (GA4 path or attribution survey), double down on those topics, write 5-10 BOFU pages next 60 days, + retrofit contextual CTAs on ur top 10 traffic posts. measure signups/post not blended conv rate

caveat: if u find some posts already convert 3%+ & just have bad CTAs, flip order & fix CTAs 1st — data b4 dogma
